description:
Advances: mutagen.cli

Deliverable:
Add a runtime preamble to Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/1 BEFORE delegating to run/2:
  1. Mix.Task.run("loadpaths") — adds host's _build/<env>/lib/<app>/ebin to :code paths
  2. Mix.Task.run("compile") — compiles host project (no-op when already compiled)
  3. Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex) — boots MutagenEx.Application
  4. ExUnit.start(autorun: false) — starts :ex_unit application

Extract to a private helper (ensure_runtime/0) so run/1 body stays readable.

run/2 stays preamble-free — test seam stays clean per mutagen.decision.preamble_in_run1_only. README's library-entry paragraph gets a one-sentence note that callers of MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1 must ensure :mutagen_ex is started themselves.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - mutagen.cli.v9 (mix test test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs --include integration) — gated by S4 landing
  - existing mix test passes with no regression
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - mutagen.cli.s14a — fresh-shell downstream invocation succeeds (currently aborts with module_beam_missing)
  - mutagen.cli.s14b — run/2 from inside mix test does NOT invoke the preamble

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex (owned by S2)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex (owned by S3)
- test/mutagen_ex/golden/*.json (owned by S2)
- test/integration/** (owned by S4)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex and any other phase implementations

Allowed touches:
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (run/1 body, optional private helper)
- test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs (additive — preamble idempotency unit test, default_dispatch shape regression guard)
- README.md (one-sentence library-callers note)
- .spec/specs/cli.spec.md (already updated by /distill — DO NOT re-edit unless adding missed coverage)

Out of Scope (intent):
- No changes to run/2's signature or behavior (the test seam)
- No new CLI flags (e.g. --skip-compile is explicitly out)
- No changes to MutagenEx.MutationRunner or any library-entry surface
- No new dependencies
- No refactor of unrelated mix task internals (resist the urge while you're in the file)

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] Spec verification: cli.r14 stub (cli.v9) referenced but execute: false until S4 lands
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

and ExUnit.start(autorun: false) before delegating to run/2. run/2 stays preamble-free

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 3 (README.md, lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex, test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 7 of 7 execute:true stubs (cli.v1, v2, v4, v5, v6, v7, v8); 3 deferred (v3, v9, v10 all execute:false). Passed: 7/7.
  - v1 (cli_test.exs): 78 tests, 0 failures
  - v2 (cli_test.exs --only exit_codes): 6 tests, 0 failures
  - v4 (mix help mutagen): rendered successfully
  - v5 (json_path_test.exs + cli_test.exs): 97 tests, 0 failures
  - v6 (cli_test.exs --only tag_charset): 11 tests, 0 failures
  - v7 (cli_test.exs + mutation_enumerator_test.exs): 105 tests, 0 failures
  - v8 (mutation_runner_test.exs + cli_test.exs): 105 tests, 0 failures
- Requirements coverage (Advances: mutagen.cli — r14 is the requirement this ticket targets):
  - mutagen.cli.r14 (preamble in run/1 only, run/2 stays clean): the "run/2 preamble-free" half is covered by test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs:31 ("run/2 does not invoke the runtime preamble"). The test installs an :erlang.trace_pattern on Mix.Task.run/1, calls Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 with stub facades (CliStub, ScopeAbort, IoStub), and refute_received {:trace, _pid, :call, {Mix.Task, :run, ["loadpaths"]}} / ["compile"]. Would fail if a future maintainer wired ensure_runtime/0 (or any Mix.Task.run("loadpaths"/"compile") call) into run/2 or any path reachable from it — the refute_received would receive a matching trace message and fail the assertion. The "run/1 actually fires the preamble" half is explicitly deferred to S4 / cli.v9 (mix test test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs --include integration, execute:false until S4 lands) per the ticket's Verification block and the Merge gate note. Source-claim trace: ensure_runtime/0 at lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:233-239 invokes the exact 4-step sequence r14 mandates (loadpaths, compile, Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex), ExUnit.start(autorun: false)) in that order; it is the only caller of run/1's preamble path (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:229), and there are no other ensure_runtime call sites (grep -rn ensure_runtime lib/ test/ returns only the definition and the run/1 callsite).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  - run/2 signature/behavior unchanged (only run/1 body received ensure_runtime() prelude; run/2 untouched)
  - No new CLI flags
  - MutagenEx.MutationRunner not touched
  - mix.exs not touched (no new deps)
  - No unrelated mix-task refactor
- Project verification: PASS
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean (rc=0)
  - mix test: 633 tests, 0 failures (17 excluded)
  - mix test --include integration (strict pre-merge): 650 tests, 0 failures
- Audit-expanded scope: none (no prior AUDIT: BOUNCE on this ticket)

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- README touch is the one-sentence library-callers note the ticket calls for (README.md:49-50).
- Coverage for s14a (fresh-shell downstream invocation positive path) is intentionally deferred to S4/cli.v9 per the ticket Verification block ("gated by S4 landing") and the Merge gate ("cli.v9 referenced but execute: false until S4 lands"). The s14b half is the testable contract of this ticket and it is covered.
